pattern: 2-D knapsack DP where each string costs (zeros, ones) and you maximize the count of strings packed within capacity (m, n).
hints: Treat m and n as two independent weight dimensions; each binary string has a weight equal to its count of 0s and 1s.
Define dp[i][j] as the largest subset size achievable using at most i zeros and j ones, and update it for each string.
Iterate strings and update the DP table from large capacities down to small (like a 1-D knapsack) to prevent counting a string twice.

edge_inputs.description: classic mixed example with best subset of size 4
two-string example where both fit under tight budget
single string that exactly fits the budget
single string that does not fit
all strings are '0', budget on ones is 0 so all fit
all strings are '1', budget on zeros is 0 so all fit
zero budget for both — no string can be selected

stress.description: large random binary string set with moderate budgets
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n_strs = max(1, size)
    strs = [''.join(rng.choice('01') for _ in range(rng.randint(1, 20))) for _ in range(n_strs)]
    m = rng.randint(30, 60)
    n = rng.randint(30, 60)
    return [strs, m, n]
stress.note: sizes within constraint limits for correct DP
